CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (1, CURRENT_TIME);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at) VALUES (2, CURRENT_TIME + INTERVAL '1 hour');
SELECT id, created_at, CURRENT_TIME FROM test_table;
SELECT id, created_at FROM test_table WHERE created_at < CURRENT_TIME - INTERVAL '1 day';
SELECT id, created_at FROM test_table WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_TIME AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE created_at < CURRENT_TIME - INTERVAL '1 hour');
CREATE TABLE additional_table (ref_id INT, updated_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO additional_table (ref_id, updated_at) VALUES (1, CURRENT_TIME - INTERVAL '15 minutes');
SELECT a.id, a.created_at, b.updated_at FROM test_table a JOIN additional_table b ON a.id = b.ref_id WHERE b.updated_at > CURRENT_TIME - INTERVAL '30 minutes';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE created_at = CURRENT_TIME;
SELECT a.id FROM test_table a WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM additional_table b WHERE b.ref_id = a.id AND b.updated_at < CURRENT_TIME);
SELECT a.id, CURRENT_TIME FROM test_table a WHERE a.created_at IS NULL OR a.created_at < CURRENT_TIME;
CREATE VIEW time_view AS SELECT id, CURRENT_TIME AS now FROM test_table;
SELECT id, created_at FROM test_table ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5;
SELECT a.id, CURRENT_TIME AS current_time_now FROM test_table a LEFT JOIN additional_table b ON a.id = b.ref_id WHERE b.updated_at IS NULL;
